mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for future equity cash flows, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off selling, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output that compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of declining INDPRO periods, or the mechanism fails to predict direction during recovery phases (accuracy < 50%).

mechanism_narrative: Tight monetary policy combined with a rapidly expanding nonfarm payrolls count creates stagflationary pressure that erodes equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 20-day returns in >58% of triggered months, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP exceeding 0.5% triggers recession fears that cause immediate portfolio de-risking, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of contraction quarters, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Declining consumer price index readings reduce the probability of further central bank tightening, lowering equity risk premiums and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of declining CPIAUCSL periods, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL changes and forward returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate below 3.5% indicates an overheated labor market that forces aggressive monetary tightening, ultimately depressing S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >62% of instances when UNRATE falls below 3.5%, or the predictive accuracy drops below 45% in full-employment regimes.
